info.title: Venice.ai API
info.x-guidance:
Venice.ai is an OpenAI-compatible inference API supporting text, image, audio, and video generation.
**Authentication options:** - API Key: Use Bearer token in Authorization header - x402 Wallet: Use USDC credits via Ethereum wallet (no account required)
**For x402 wallet access:** 1. POST /x402/top-up without headers to get payment requirements 2. Sign a USDC payment using the x402 SDK 3. POST /x402/top-up with X-402-Payment header to add credits 4. Call any inference endpoint with X-Sign-In-With-X header
**Pricing:** Prepaid credits consumed per request. Check /models for available models and their capabilities.

tags.description: Generate speech/audio, transcribe audio, and manage asynchronous audio generation jobs.
tags.name: Audio

tags.description: Given a list of messages comprising a conversation, the model will return a response. Supports multimodal inputs including text, images, audio (input_audio), and video (video_url) for compatible models.
tags.name: Chat

tags.description: List and describe the various models available in the API.
tags.name: Models

tags.description: Generate and manipulate images using AI models.
tags.name: Image

tags.description: List and retrieve character information for use in completions.
tags.name: Characters

tags.description: Billing and usage analytics. **Beta**: This API is currently in beta and may be unstable. Endpoints, request/response schemas, and behavior may change without notice.
tags.name: Billing

tags.description: Proxy JSON-RPC requests to blockchain nodes. Billed per credit.
tags.name: Crypto RPC

tags.description:
Wallet-based API access using the x402 protocol. No API key required — authenticate with an Ethereum wallet.
**How it works:** 1. **Authenticate** — Send an `X-Sign-In-With-X` header (base64-encoded signed SIWE message) with any request. See the `siwx` security scheme for the exact format. 2. **Top up** — `POST /x402/top-up` without a payment header returns payment requirements. Sign a USDC transfer on Base using the x402 SDK (`npm install x402`) and re-submit with the `X-402-Payment` header. 3. **Use any endpoint** — All inference endpoints (chat, image, audio, video, embeddings) accept `siwx` as an alternative to `BearerAuth`. Charges are deducted from your USDC credit balance. 4. **Monitor balance** — `GET /x402/balance/{walletAddress}` returns your current balance. The `X-Balance-Remaining` response header on inference calls also reports it.
**Quick start (5 lines):** ``` import { VeniceClient } from '@venice-ai/x402-client' const venice = new VeniceClient(process.env.WALLET_KEY) await venice.topUp(10) // $10 USDC on Base const res = await venice.chat({ model: 'zai-org-glm-5-1', messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Hello!' }] }) ```
**Payment:** USDC on Base (chain ID 8453). Minimum top-up: $5. Alternatively, stake DIEM tokens for daily credits (1 DIEM = $1/day).
tags.name: x402

siwx.description:
Wallet-based authentication using the x402 protocol (Sign-In-With-X / EIP-4361 SIWE).
**Header format:** Base64-encoded JSON object with the following fields: - `address` — Ethereum wallet address (checksummed) - `message` — EIP-4361 SIWE message string (created with `SiweMessage.prepareMessage()`) - `signature` — Hex-encoded signature of the message, signed by the wallet's private key - `timestamp` — Unix timestamp in milliseconds - `chainId` — Chain ID (use `8453` for Base)
**SIWE message fields:** - `domain`: `outerface.venice.ai` - `uri`: `https://outerface.venice.ai` - `version`: `"1"` - `chainId`: `8453` - `nonce`: Random 16-character hex string - `issuedAt` / `expirationTime`: ISO timestamps (recommended TTL: 10 minutes) - `statement`: `"Sign in to Venice API"`
**Example (TypeScript):** ``` import { Wallet } from 'ethers' import { SiweMessage } from 'siwe'
const wallet = new Wallet(PRIVATE_KEY) const msg = new SiweMessage({ domain: 'outerface.venice.ai', address: wallet.address, statement: 'Sign in to Venice API', uri: 'https://outerface.venice.ai', version: '1', chainId: 8453, nonce: crypto.randomUUID().replace(/-/g, '').slice(0, 16), issuedAt: new Date().toISOString(), expirationTime: new Date(Date.now() + 600000).toISOString() }) const signature = await wallet.signMessage(msg.prepareMessage()) const header = btoa(JSON.stringify({ address: wallet.address, message: msg.prepareMessage(), signature, timestamp: Date.now(), chainId: 8453 })) // Set header: X-Sign-In-With-X: <header> ```
**SDK:** `npm install @venice-ai/x402-client` provides `VeniceClient` and `createAuthFetch` which handle this automatically.
**Billing:** x402 users pay from a prepaid USDC credit balance. Top up via `POST /x402/top-up`. When balance is insufficient, endpoints return `402` with structured top-up instructions.
siwx.name: X-Sign-In-With-X

suggested_prompt.description: An optional provider-suggested replacement prompt that complies with content policy.
suggested_prompt.example: A cinematic instrumental track inspired by stormy weather and dramatic tension.

error.example: File exceeds the maximum allowed size of 25 MB.

error.description: Error message indicating payment is required.

message.description: Human-readable context about the payment requirement.

suggestedTopUpUsd.description: Suggested amount to top up in USD.

minimumTopUpUsd.description: Minimum allowed top-up amount in USD.

supportedTokens.description: List of supported token symbols for payment.

step1.example: POST /api/v1/x402/top-up with no payment header to get payment requirements

step2.example: Sign a USDC transfer authorization using the x402 SDK (createPaymentHeader)

step3.example: POST /api/v1/x402/top-up with the signed X-402-Payment header

issuedAt.description: ISO timestamp when the challenge was issued.

expirationTime.description: ISO timestamp when the challenge expires.

statement.example: Sign in with your wallet to access Venice x402 API

frequency_penalty.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.

logprobs.description: Whether to include log probabilities in the response. This is not supported by all models.

top_logprobs.description: The number of highest probability tokens to return for each token position.

max_completion_tokens.description: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a completion, including visible output tokens and reasoning tokens.

max_temp.description: Maximum temperature value for dynamic temperature scaling.

max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the chat completion. This value can be used to control costs for text generated via API. Values of 0 or less are ignored and the model will use its default maximum. This value is now deprecated in favor of max_completion_tokens.

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

text.description: The prompt text of the message. Must be at-least one character in length
text.title: Text Content Object

oneOf.description: Text message type. Supports optional cache_control for prompt caching on supported providers.
oneOf.title: text

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

url.description: The URL of the image. Can be a data URL with a base64 encoded image or a public URL. URL must be publicly accessible. Image must pass validation checks and be >= 64 pixels square.

image_url.description: Object containing the image URL information
image_url.title: Image URL Object

oneOf.description: Image URL message type. Supports optional cache_control for prompt caching on supported providers.
oneOf.title: image_url

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

data.description: Base64-encoded audio data. Direct URLs are not supported for audio content.

format.description: The format of the audio file. Common formats include wav, mp3, aac, ogg, flac, m4a. Defaults to wav.

input_audio.description: Object containing the base64-encoded audio data and format
input_audio.title: Input Audio Object

oneOf.description: Audio input message type for models that support audio analysis. Audio must be base64-encoded.
oneOf.title: input_audio

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

url.description: The URL of the video. Can be a direct URL (including YouTube links for some providers), or a base64-encoded data URL (e.g., data:video/mp4;base64,...). Supported formats: mp4, mpeg, mov, webm.

video_url.description: Object containing the video URL information
video_url.title: Video URL Object

oneOf.description: Video URL message type for models that support video analysis. Supports both direct URLs and base64-encoded data URLs.
oneOf.title: video_url

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

file_data.description: The file content as a data URL (e.g., data:application/pdf;base64,...) or a publicly accessible URL. Supported formats: PDF, EPUB, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, XLS, plain text, Markdown, CSV, JSON, and most source-code files (e.g., .py, .js, .ts, .c, .cpp, .java, .go, .rs, .ps1, .sh, .yaml, .sql).

filename.description: Optional filename for the file. Used for display purposes.

file.description: Object containing the file data and optional filename
file.title: File Object

oneOf.description: File input message type for document processing. Supports PDF, EPUB, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, plain text, and most source-code files (e.g., .py, .js, .ts, .c, .cpp, .java, .go, .rs, .ps1, .sh, .yaml, .sql). Files are extracted to text before being sent to the model.
oneOf.title: file

anyOf.description: The user message is the input from the user. It is part of the conversation and is visible to the assistant.
anyOf.title: User Message

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

text.title: Text Content Object

items.title: text

reasoning_details.description: Reasoning details returned by certain reasoning models that support this feature (e.g., Gemini 3 Pro). Not all reasoning models return this field. For multi-turn conversations with tool calls on supported models, pass back the reasoning_details exactly as received to preserve thought signatures.

anyOf.description: The assistant message contains the response from the LLM. Must have either content or tool_calls.
anyOf.title: Assistant Message

anyOf.description: The tool message is a special message that is used to call a tool. It is not part of the conversation and is not visible to the user.
anyOf.title: Tool Message

text.title: Text Content Object

items.description: Text message type. Supports optional cache_control for prompt caching on supported providers.
items.title: text

anyOf.description: The system message is a special message that provides context to the model. It is not part of the conversation and is not visible to the user.
anyOf.title: System Message

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

text.description: The prompt text of the message. Must be at-least one character in length
text.title: Text Content Object

items.description: Text message type. Supports optional cache_control for prompt caching on supported providers.
items.title: text

anyOf.description: The developer message provides high-level instructions to reasoning models (o1, codex). Semantically similar to system messages but specifically designed for reasoning model workflows.
anyOf.title: Developer Message

messages.description: A list of messages comprising the conversation so far. Depending on the model you use, different message types (modalities) are supported, like text and images. Non-multimodal models reject image content. For vision models that support multiple images (supportsMultipleImages), images are preserved across all messages in the conversation history. For single-image vision models, only the last image-containing message retains its images.

min_p.description: Sets a minimum probability threshold for token selection. Tokens with probabilities below this value are filtered out.

min_temp.description: Minimum temperature value for dynamic temperature scaling.

model.description: The ID of the model you wish to prompt. May also be a model trait, or a model compatibility mapping. See the models endpoint for a list of models available to you. You can use feature suffixes to enable features from the venice_parameters object. Please see "Model Feature Suffix" documentation for more details.

n.description: How many chat completion choices to generate for each input message. Note that you will be charged based on the number of generated tokens across all of the choices. Keep n as 1 to minimize costs.

presence_penalty.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood to talk about new topics.

prompt_cache_key.description: When supplied, this field may be used to optimize conversation routing to improve cache performance and thus reduce latency.

prompt_cache_retention.description: OpenAI-compatible parameter to control prompt cache retention. "extended" or "24h" extends retention to 24 hours for supported models.

repetition_penalty.description: The parameter for repetition penalty. 1.0 means no penalty. Values > 1.0 discourage repetition.

effort.description: Controls the reasoning effort level for supported models. Higher effort means more thorough reasoning but increased token usage. Defaults to the model configuration if not specified.

summary.description: Controls whether and how the model generates a summary of its reasoning. "auto" lets the model decide, "concise" requests a brief summary, "detailed" requests a thorough summary.

reasoning.description: Configuration for reasoning behavior on supported models.

reasoning_effort.description: OpenAI-compatible parameter to control reasoning effort level for supported models. Takes precedence over reasoning.effort if both are provided.

seed.description: The random seed used to generate the response. This is useful for reproducibility.

stop.description: Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens. Defaults to null.

stop_token_ids.description: Array of token IDs where the API will stop generating further tokens.

stream.description: Whether to stream back partial progress. Defaults to false.

include_usage.description: Whether to include usage information in the stream.

temperature.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic. We generally recommend altering this or top_p but not both.

top_k.description: The number of highest probability vocabulary tokens to keep for top-k-filtering.

top_p.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.

user.description: This field is discarded on the request but is supported in the Venice API for compatibility with OpenAI clients.

store.description: This field is accepted for OpenAI compatibility but is not used by Venice.

verbosity.description: Controls the verbosity of the text response.

include.description: OpenAI-compatible parameter specifying additional data to include in the response.

metadata.description: OpenAI-compatible metadata parameter for request tracking.

character_slug.description: The character slug of a public Venice character. Discoverable as the "Public ID" on the published character page.

strip_thinking_response.description: Strip <think></think> blocks from the response. Applicable only to reasoning / thinking models. Also available to use as a model feature suffix. Defaults to false.

disable_thinking.description: On supported reasoning models, will disable thinking and strip the <think></think> blocks from the response. Defaults to false.

enable_e2ee.description: Enable end-to-end encryption for E2EE-capable models. When true (default), E2EE is used if E2EE headers are present. When false, the model runs in TEE-only mode even if E2EE headers are present. Only applicable to models with E2EE capability.

enable_web_search.description: Enable web search for this request. Defaults to off. On will force web search on the request. Auto will enable it based on the model's discretion. Citations will be returned either in the first chunk of a streaming result, or in the non streaming response.

enable_web_scraping.description: Enable Venice web scraping of URLs in the latest user message using Firecrawl. Off by default.

enable_web_citations.description: When web search is enabled, this will request that the LLM cite its sources using a ^index^ or ^i,j^ superscript format (e.g., ^1^). Defaults to false.

include_search_results_in_stream.description: Experimental feature - When set to true, the LLM will include search results in the stream as the first emitted chunk. Defaults to false.

return_search_results_as_documents.description: When set, search results are also surfaced in an OpenAI-compatible tool call named "venice_web_search_documents" to ease LangChain consumption.

include_venice_system_prompt.description: Whether to include the Venice supplied system prompts along side specified system prompts. Defaults to true.

enable_x_search.description: Enable xAI native search (web + X/Twitter) for supported models. When enabled, the model performs web and X searches server-side instead of Venice search augmentation. Only available on models with supportsXSearch capability (e.g., grok-4-20). Additional per-search charges apply (~$0.01/search).

venice_parameters.description: Unique parameters to Venice's API implementation. Customize these to control the behavior of the model.

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to enable parallel function calling during tool use.

oneOf.description: The JSON Schema that should be used to validate and format the response.
oneOf.title: json_schema

oneOf.description: The response should be formatted as a JSON object. This is a deprecated implementation and the preferred use is json_schema.
oneOf.title: json_object

oneOf.description: The response should be returned as plain text. This is the default behavior.
oneOf.title: text

response_format.description: Format in which the response should be returned.

strict.description: If set to true, the model will follow the exact schema defined in the parameters field. Only a subset of JSON Schema is supported when strict is true.

items.description: A tool that can be called by the model. Currently, only functions are supported as tools.
items.title: Tool Call

tools.description: A list of tools the model may call. Currently, only functions are supported as a tool. Use this to provide a list of functions the model may generate JSON inputs for.

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

data.description: Base64-encoded audio data. Direct URLs are not supported for audio content.

format.description: The format of the audio file. Common formats include wav, mp3, aac, ogg, flac, m4a. Defaults to wav.

input_audio.description: Object containing the base64-encoded audio data and format
input_audio.title: Input Audio Object

ChatCompletionContentPartInputAudio.description: Audio input message type for models that support audio analysis. Audio must be base64-encoded.
ChatCompletionContentPartInputAudio.title: input_audio

ttl.description: Optional TTL for extended cache duration. Beta feature requiring special header.

type.description: The type of cache control. Currently only "ephemeral" is supported.

cache_control.description: Optional cache control for prompt caching on supported providers.
cache_control.title: Cache Control

url.description: The URL of the video. Can be a direct URL (including YouTube links for some providers), or a base64-encoded data URL (e.g., data:video/mp4;base64,...). Supported formats: mp4, mpeg, mov, webm.

video_url.description: Object containing the video URL information
video_url.title: Video URL Object

ChatCompletionContentPartVideoUrl.description: Video URL message type for models that support video analysis. Supports both direct URLs and base64-encoded data URLs.
ChatCompletionContentPartVideoUrl.title: video_url

model.description: The ID of the model to use. E2EE-capable models are not supported on /api/v1/responses; use /api/v1/chat/completions with the required E2EE headers instead.

input.description: The input to generate a response for. Can be a simple string or an array of messages.

max_output_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate.

effort.description: Controls reasoning effort level for supported models.

tools.description: A list of tools the model may call.

tool_choice.description: Controls which tool is called by the model.

web_search.description: Enable web search for this request.

stream.description: Whether to stream back partial progress.

character_slug.description: The character slug of a public Venice character.

enable_e2ee.description: Enable end-to-end encryption for E2EE-capable models. When true (default), E2EE is used if headers are present. When false, TEE-only mode is used.

enable_web_search.description: Enable web search for this request.

enable_web_scraping.description: Enable Venice web scraping of URLs in the latest user message.

enable_web_citations.description: Request that the LLM cite its sources.

include_venice_system_prompt.description: Whether to include the Venice supplied system prompts.

include_search_results_in_stream.description: Include search results in the stream as the first emitted chunk.

ResponsesRequest.description: Request body for the Responses API endpoint. E2EE-capable models are not supported on /api/v1/responses; use /api/v1/chat/completions with the required E2EE headers instead.
ResponsesRequest.title: Responses API Request

created_at.description: Unix timestamp of when the response was created.

model.description: The model used for the response.

output.description: The output items generated by the model.

error.description: Error information if the response failed.
error.title: Error

ResponsesResponse.description: Response from the Responses API endpoint.
ResponsesResponse.title: Responses API Response

cfg_scale.description: CFG scale parameter. Higher values lead to more adherence to the prompt.

embed_exif_metadata.description: Embed prompt generation information into the image's EXIF metadata.

format.description: The image format to return. WebP are smaller and optimized for web use. PNG are higher quality but larger in file size.

hide_watermark.description: Whether to hide the Venice watermark. Venice may ignore this parameter for certain generated content.

inpaint.description: This feature is deprecated and was disabled on May 19th, 2025. A revised in-painting API will be launched in the near future.

lora_strength.description: Lora strength for the model. Only applies if the model uses additional Loras.

model.description: The model to use for image generation.

negative_prompt.description: A description of what should not be in the image. Character limit is model specific and is listed in the promptCharacterLimit constraint in the model list endpoint.

prompt.description: The description for the image. Character limit is model specific and is listed in the promptCharacterLimit setting in the model list endpoint.
prompt.example: A beautiful sunset over a mountain range

return_binary.description: Whether to return binary image data instead of base64.

variants.description: Number of images to generate (1–4). Only supported when return_binary is false.

safe_mode.description: Whether to use safe mode. If enabled, this will blur images that are classified as having adult content.

seed.description: Random seed for generation. If not provided, a random seed will be used.

steps.description: Number of inference steps. This model does not support steps - this field is ignored.

style_preset.description: An image style to apply to the image. Visit https://docs.venice.ai/api-reference/endpoint/image/styles for more details.

aspect_ratio.description: Aspect ratio (utilized by certain image models including Nano Banana). Examples: "1:1", "16:9".

resolution.description: Resolution (utilized by certain image models including Nano Banana). Examples: "1K", "2K", "4K".

enable_web_search.description: Enable web search for the image generation task. This will allow the model to use the latest information from the web to generate the image. Only supported by certain models. If web search is used, additional credits are getting charged.

background.description: This parameter is not used in Venice image generation but is supported for compatibility with OpenAI API

model.description: The model to use for image generation. Defaults to Venice's default image model. If a non-existent model is specified (ie an OpenAI model name), it will default to Venice's default image model.

moderation.description: auto enables safe venice mode which will blur out adult content. low disables safe venice mode.

n.description: Number of images to generate. Venice presently only supports 1 image per request.

output_compression.description: This parameter is not used in Venice image generation but is supported for compatibility with OpenAI API

prompt.description: A text description of the desired image.
prompt.example: A beautiful sunset over mountain ranges

quality.description: This parameter is not used in Venice image generation but is supported for compatibility with OpenAI API

response_format.description: Response format. URL will be a data URL.

size.description: Size of generated images. Default is 1024x1024

style.description: This parameter is not used in Venice image generation but is supported for compatibility with OpenAI API

user.description: This parameter is not used in Venice image generation but is supported for compatibility with OpenAI API

enhance.description: Whether to enhance the image using Venice's image engine during upscaling. Must be true if scale is 1.

enhanceCreativity.description: Higher values let the enhancement AI change the image more. Setting this to 1 effectively creates an entirely new image.

enhancePrompt.description: The text to image style to apply during prompt enhancement. Does best with short descriptive prompts, like gold, marble or angry, menacing.

image.description: The image to upscale. Can be either a file upload or a base64-encoded string. Image dimensions must be at least 65536 pixels and final dimensions after scaling must not exceed 16777216 pixels. File size must be less than 25MB.

replication.description: How strongly lines and noise in the base image are preserved. Higher values are noisier but less plastic/AI "generated"/hallucinated. Must be between 0 and 1.

scale.description: The scale factor for upscaling the image. Must be a number between 1 and 4. Scale of 1 requires enhance to be set true and will only run the enhancer. Scale must be > 1 if enhance is false. A scale of 4 with large images will result in the scale being dynamically set to ensure the final image stays within the maximum size limits.

UpscaleImageRequest.description: Upscale or enhance an image based on the supplied parameters. Using a scale of 1 with enhance enabled will only run the enhancer.

aspect_ratio.description: The aspect ratio for the output image. Omit this parameter to use the model's default setting. Supported values vary by model - check GET /api/v1/models for model-specific options.

resolution.description: Resolution tier for the output image (e.g. "1K", "2K", "4K"). Supported values vary by model - check GET /api/v1/models for model-specific options. Defaults to "1K" when not specified.

image.description: The image to edit. Can be either a file upload, a base64-encoded string, or a URL starting with http:// or https://. Image dimensions must be at least 65536 pixels and must not exceed 33177600 pixels. File size must be less than 25MB.

model.description: The model ID to use for image editing.

modelId.description: Deprecated: Use "model" instead. The model ID to use for image editing.

prompt.description: The text directions to edit or modify the image. Short, descriptive prompts work best (e.g., "remove the tree", "change the sky to sunrise"). Character limit is model specific and is listed in the promptCharacterLimit setting in the model list endpoint.
prompt.example: Change the color of the sky to a sunrise

safe_mode.description: Whether to use safe mode. If enabled, this will blur images that are classified as having adult content.

EditImageRequest.description: Edit an image based on the supplied prompt.

modelId.description: The model ID to use for multi-edit.

prompt.description: The text directions to edit or modify the image. Short, descriptive prompts work best (e.g., "remove the tree", "change the sky to sunrise"). Character limit is model specific and is listed in the promptCharacterLimit setting in the model list endpoint.

items.description: Image as a base64-encoded string or a URL starting with http:// or https://

images.description: Array of 1 to 3 images used for multi-editing. The first image is treated as the base image, and the remaining images are used as edit layers/masks. Each image can be a base64-encoded string or a URL starting with http:// or https://. Image dimensions must be at least 65536 pixels and must not exceed 33177600 pixels. File size must be less than 25MB.

resolution.description: Resolution tier for the output image (e.g. "1K", "2K", "4K"). Supported values vary by model - check GET /api/v1/models for model-specific options. Defaults to "1K" when not specified.

safe_mode.description: Whether to use safe mode. If enabled, this will blur images that are classified as having adult content.

MultiEditImageRequest.description: Edit an image by compositing up to three layered images with a single prompt. Supports base64-encoded strings and URLs.

modelId.description: The model ID to use for multi-edit.

prompt.description: The text directions to edit or modify the image. Short, descriptive prompts work best (e.g., "remove the tree", "change the sky to sunrise"). Character limit is model specific and is listed in the promptCharacterLimit setting in the model list endpoint.

images.description: Array of 1 to 3 image files. The first image is treated as the base image, and the remaining images are used as edit layers/masks.

resolution.description: Resolution tier for the output image (e.g. "1K", "2K", "4K"). Supported values vary by model - check GET /api/v1/models for model-specific options. Defaults to "1K" when not specified.

safe_mode.description: Whether to use safe mode. If enabled, this will blur images that are classified as having adult content.

MultiEditImageMultipartRequest.description: Edit an image by compositing up to three layered image files with a single prompt. Only file uploads are supported with multipart/form-data.

image.description: The image to remove the background from. Can be either a file upload or a base64-encoded string. File size must be less than 25MB.

image_url.description: URL of the image to remove the background from.

BackgroundRemoveImageRequest.description: Remove the background from an image. Provide either an image file/base64 or an image URL.

dimensions.description: The number of dimensions the resulting output embeddings should have.

encoding_format.description: The format to return the embeddings in. Can be either `float` or `base64`.

anyOf.description: The string that will be turned into an embedding. Cannot be an empty string.
anyOf.title: string

anyOf.description: The array of strings that will be turned into an embedding. Array must be 2048 dimensions or less.
anyOf.title: array

anyOf.description: The array of integers that will be turned into an embedding. Array must be 2048 dimensions or less.
anyOf.title: array

anyOf.description: The array of arrays containing integers that will be turned into an embedding. Array must be 2048 dimensions or less.
anyOf.title: array

input.description: Input text to embed, encoded as a string or array of tokens. To embed multiple inputs in a single request, pass an array of strings or array of token arrays. The input must not exceed the max input tokens for the model (8192 tokens), cannot be an empty string, and any array must be 2048 dimensions or less.
input.example: The quick brown fox jumped over the lazy dog

model.description: ID of the model to use. You can use the List models API to see all of your available models, or see our Model overview for descriptions of them.

user.description: This is an unused parameter and is discarded by Venice. It is supported solely for API compatibility with OpenAI.

CreateEmbeddingRequestSchema.description: Create embeddings for the supplied input.

example.input: The quick brown fox jumped over the lazy dog

input.description: The text to generate audio for. The maximum length is 4096 characters.
input.example: Hello, this is a test of the text to speech system.

language.description: Optional language hint. Accepted values are model-specific: Qwen 3 accepts full names (English, Chinese, ...); xAI/ElevenLabs accept ISO 639-1 codes (en, ja, ...); MiniMax accepts full names. Unsupported values are silently ignored. Omit to let the model auto-detect.

model.description: The model ID of a Venice TTS model.

prompt.description: A style prompt to control the emotion and delivery of the speech. Supported by models advertising `supportsPromptParam` (currently Qwen 3 TTS). Ignored by other models. Examples: "Very happy.", "Sad and slow.", "Excited and energetic."

speed.description: The speed of the generated audio. Select a value from 0.25 to 4.0. 1.0 is the default.

streaming.description: Should the content stream back sentence by sentence or be processed and returned as a complete audio file.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature for speech generation. Higher values produce more varied output. Supported by models advertising `supportsTemperatureParam` (Qwen 3, Orpheus, Chatterbox HD). Ignored by other models.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling parameter. Supported by models advertising `supportsTopPParam` (currently Qwen 3 TTS). Ignored by other models.

voice.description: The voice to use when generating the audio. Voices are model-specific: Kokoro (e.g. af_sky, af_bella, am_adam), Qwen 3 (e.g. Vivian, Serena, Dylan), xAI (eve, ara, rex, sal, leo), Orpheus (tara, leah, jess, leo, dan, mia, zac, zoe), Inworld (Craig, Ashley, ...), Chatterbox (Aurora, Blade, ...), ElevenLabs Turbo (Rachel, Aria, ...), MiniMax (WiseWoman, DeepVoiceMan, ...). You can also pass a cloned-voice handle (`vv_<id>`) returned by POST /v1/audio/voices to synthesize in a previously cloned voice; the handle must be paired with the same model used to create it. Using an incompatible voice returns a 400 error. Call GET /models/{id} to list voices for a specific model.

CreateSpeechRequestSchema.description: Request to generate audio from text.

file.description: The audio file object (not a base64 string). Supported formats: WAV, WAVE, FLAC, M4A, AAC, MP4, MP3, OGG, OGA, WEBM.

model.description: The model to use for transcription. See https://docs.venice.ai/models/overview for more information.

response_format.description: The format of the transcript output, in one of these options: json, text.

timestamps.description: Whether to include timestamps in the response.

language.description: ISO 639-1 language code (e.g., "en", "es", "fr"). Optional - if not provided, the model will auto-detect the language. Note: Only supported by certain models (e.g., Whisper). Ignored by models that do not support language hints.

CreateTranscriptionRequestSchema.description: Request to transcribe audio to text.

file.description: The voice sample audio file (multipart/form-data, field name "file"). Accepted containers depend on the selected model: `tts-chatterbox-hd` accepts MP3, WAV, FLAC, and M4A; `tts-minimax-speech-02-hd` accepts MP3 and WAV only. Recommended: a clean speech recording of at least 5–10 seconds.

model.description: The Venice TTS model the cloned voice will be paired with. The returned voice handle is only valid against this model on POST /v1/audio/speech.

CreateClonedVoiceRequestSchema.description: Request to create a cloned voice from an audio sample. The returned `vv_<id>` handle is passed back as the `voice` parameter on POST /v1/audio/speech.

model.description: The model to use for video generation.

prompt.description: The prompt to use for video generation. Required for most models. The maximum length varies by model (default 2500 characters, up to 10000 for some models such as Seedance 2.0).
prompt.example: Commerce being conducted in the city of Venice, Italy.

negative_prompt.description: Optional negative prompt. The maximum length varies by model (default 2500 characters, up to 10000 for some models).
negative_prompt.example: low resolution, error, worst quality, low quality, defects

duration.description: The duration of the video to generate. Available options vary by model.

aspect_ratio.description: The aspect ratio of the video. Available options vary by model. Some models do not support aspect_ratio.

resolution.description: The resolution of the video. Available options vary by model. Some models do not support resolution. Use upscale_factor for upscale models.

upscale_factor.description: For upscale models only. 1 = quality enhancement, 2 = double resolution (default), 4 = quadruple.

audio.description: For models which support audio generation and configuration. Defaults to true.

image_url.description: For image-to-video models, the reference image. Must be a URL (http/https) or a data URL (data:image/...).

end_image_url.description: For models that support end images or transitions, the end frame image. Must be a URL or data URL.

audio_url.description: For models that support audio input, background music. Must be a URL or data URL. Supported: WAV, MP3. Max: 30s, 15MB.

video_url.description: For models that support video input (video-to-video, upscale). Must be a URL or data URL. Supported: MP4, MOV, WebM.

reference_image_urls.description: For models with reference image support, up to 9 images for character/style consistency. Each must be a URL or data URL.

elements.description: For models with advanced element support (e.g., Kling O3 R2V). Up to 4 elements defining characters/objects. Reference in prompt as @Element1, @Element2, etc.

scene_image_urls.description: For models with advanced element support. Up to 4 scene reference images. Reference in prompt as @Image1, @Image2, etc.

QueueVideoRequest.description: Request body for video generation. Available fields and valid values vary by model.

model.description: The model to get a price quote for.

duration.description: The duration of the video. Available options vary by model.

aspect_ratio.description: The aspect ratio. Required for some models with megapixel-rate pricing.

resolution.description: The resolution. Required for models with duration-resolution-rate pricing.

audio.description: For models which support audio generation and configuration. Defaults to true.

video_url.description: For upscale models, the video to upscale. Required to auto-detect duration for pricing.

QuoteVideoRequest.description: Request body for video price quote. Available fields and valid values vary by model.

model.description: The ID of the model used for video generation.

queue_id.description: The ID of the video generation request.

model.description: The ID of the model used for video generation.

queue_id.description: The ID of the video generation request.

delete_media_on_completion.description: If true, the video media will be deleted from storage after the request is completed. If false, you can use the complete endpoint to remove the media once you have successfully downloaded the video.

response_format.description: The format of the transcript output, in one of these options: json, text.

CreateVideoTranscriptionRequestSchema.description: Request to transcribe a YouTube video URL to text.

file.description: The document file to parse. Supported formats: PDF, EPUB, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, plain text, Markdown, CSV, JSON, and most source-code files (e.g., .py, .js, .ts, .c, .cpp, .java, .go, .rs, .ps1, .sh, .yaml, .sql). Maximum size: 25MB.

response_format.description: The format of the response output. "json" returns structured JSON, "text" returns only the extracted text.

TextParserRequest.description: Request to parse and extract text from a document file.

text.description: The extracted text content from the document.

tokens.description: The token count of the extracted text.

TextParserResponse.description: Text parser response containing extracted text and token count.

model.description: The model to use for audio generation.

prompt.description: The prompt describing the audio to generate. Minimum and maximum prompt lengths vary by model; inspect `/models` for `min_prompt_length` and `prompt_character_limit`.
prompt.example: A warm spoken narration introducing a product launch.

lyrics_prompt.description: Optional lyrics/text for lyric-capable models. Required when `/models` reports `lyrics_required=true`; unsupported when `/models` reports `supports_lyrics=false`.
lyrics_prompt.example: Verse 1: Walking through the city lights...

duration_seconds.description: Optional duration hint in seconds. Only supported for models that expose duration metadata via `/models`. Accepts either an integer or a numeric string. If omitted, the model default duration is used when available.

force_instrumental.description: Optional instrumental toggle. Only supported when `/models` reports `supports_force_instrumental=true`.

lyrics_optimizer.description: When enabled, auto-generates lyrics from the prompt. Only supported when `/models` reports `supports_lyrics_optimizer=true`. lyrics_prompt must be empty when this is true.

voice.description: Optional voice selection for voice-enabled models. See `/models?type=music` for the model's supported `voices` and `default_voice`.

language_code.description: Optional ISO 639-1 language code. Only supported when `/models` reports `supports_language_code=true`.

speed.description: Optional audio speed multiplier. Only supported when `/models` reports `supports_speed=true`; use the model-specific `min_speed` and `max_speed` values.

model.description: The model to get a price quote for.

duration_seconds.description: Optional duration hint in seconds. Only supported for models that expose duration metadata via `/models`. Accepts either an integer or a numeric string. If omitted, the model default duration is used when available.

character_count.description: Optional character count for character-based pricing models. Required when the selected model uses `pricing.per_thousand_characters` in `/models`.

model.description: The ID of the model used for audio generation.

queue_id.description: The ID of the audio generation request. Use this to poll for status and retrieve the result.

model.description: The ID of the model used for audio generation.

queue_id.description: The ID of the audio generation request. Use this to poll for status and retrieve the result.

delete_media_on_completion.description: If true, the audio media will be deleted from storage after the request is completed. If false, you can use the complete endpoint to remove the media once you have successfully downloaded the audio.

canConsume.description: Whether the user has sufficient balance to make API requests

consumptionCurrency.description: The currency that will be used for consumption (DIEM or USD)

diem.description: Remaining DIEM balance for current epoch. Null if not staking.

usd.description: Remaining USD balance. Null if not available.

diemEpochAllocation.description: Total DIEM allocation for the current epoch (from staking). Use with balances.diem to calculate usage percentage.

BillingBalanceResponse.description: Current balance information for the authenticated user

warningMessage.description: A warning message to disambiguate DIEM usage from legacy DIEM (formerly VCU) usage

amount.description: The total amount charged for the billing usage entry

currency.description: The currency charged for the billing usage entry

completionTokens.description: Number of tokens used in the completion. Only present for LLM usage.

inferenceExecutionTime.description: Time taken for inference execution in milliseconds

promptTokens.description: Number of tokens requested in the prompt. Only present for LLM usage.

requestId.description: Unique identifier for the inference request

inferenceDetails.description: Details about the related inference request, if applicable

notes.description: Notes about the billing usage entry

pricePerUnitUsd.description: The price per unit in USD

sku.description: The product associated with the billing usage entry

timestamp.description: The timestamp the billing usage entry was created

BillingUsageResponse.description: The response schema for the billing usage endpoint

lookback.description: Lookback period for usage data. Format: number followed by "d" (e.g., "7d", "30d"). Maximum: 90d

startDate.description: Start date for filtering records (YYYY-MM-DD). If provided, endDate is also required.

endDate.description: End date for filtering records (YYYY-MM-DD). If provided, startDate is also required.

lookback.description: The lookback period used for the query. Either "Nd" format or "startDate:endDate" format.

USD.description: Total USD usage for this date

DIEM.description: Total DIEM usage for this date

byDate.description: Daily usage totals for the requested period

unitType.description: Type of units (tokens, images, chars, minutes, seconds)

modelType.description: Type of model (LLM, IMAGE, TTS, ASR, VIDEO)

totalUsd.description: Total USD usage for this model

totalDiem.description: Total DIEM usage for this model

totalUnits.description: Total units consumed for this model

units.description: Number of units for this breakdown

breakdown.description: Breakdown by token type (only present if multiple types)

byModel.description: Usage breakdown by model, sorted by total spend (highest first)

byModelDaily.description: Daily chart data for top 8 models. Each entry has "date" (timestamp) plus model names as keys.

topModels.description: Names of the top 8 models by usage (for chart legends)

apiKeyId.description: API key ID, or null if usage was from web app

totalUsd.description: Total USD usage for this key

totalDiem.description: Total DIEM usage for this key

totalUnits.description: Total units consumed for this key

byKey.description: Usage breakdown by API key, sorted by total spend (highest first)

byKeyDaily.description: Daily chart data for top 8 API keys. Each entry has "date" (timestamp) plus key descriptions as keys.

topKeyNames.description: Descriptions of the top 8 API keys by usage (for chart legends)

BillingUsageAnalyticsResponse.description: Aggregated usage analytics response with breakdowns by date, model, and API key

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10, max: 20)

search_provider.description: Search provider to use. "brave" uses Brave Search with Zero Data Retention (ZDR) for maximum privacy — search queries are never stored or logged. "google" uses Google Search with anonymized queries — searches are proxied through Venice's infrastructure so that your identity is not associated with the search request sent to Google. Venice does not store or log search queries. Defaults to "brave".

context_length.description: The context length (maximum input tokens) supported by the model. Only present for text models. This is a standard OpenAI-compatible field that mirrors model_spec.availableContextTokens for client compatibility.

availableContextTokens.description: The context length supported by the model. Only applicable for text models.

maxCompletionTokens.description: The maximum number of completion tokens the model can generate. Use this to know the upper bound for the max_completion_tokens request parameter. Only applicable for text models.

beta.description: Is this model restricted to beta users only? If true, only users with beta access can use this model

betaModel.description: Is this model in beta status?

privacy.description: The privacy mode of the model. Private models have zero data retention. Anonymized models Venice can not guarantee privacy on, but requests are not affiliated with a user

regionRestrictions.description: Country codes where this model is intended to be available. Only present for models with region restrictions metadata.

date.description: The ISO 8601 date when this model is scheduled to be deprecated

deprecation.description: Deprecation information for the model. Only present for models scheduled to be retired

optimizedForCode.description: Is the LLM optimized for coding?

quantization.description: The quantization type of the running model.

supportsFunctionCalling.description: Does the LLM model support function calling?

supportsAudioInput.description: Does the LLM support audio input?

supportsReasoning.description: Does the model support reasoning with <thinking> blocks of output.

supportsReasoningEffort.description: Does the model support the reasoning_effort parameter to control reasoning depth.

reasoningEffortOptions.description: Supported reasoning_effort values for this model. Only present when supportsReasoningEffort is true. The "none" option means reasoning can be disabled.

defaultReasoningEffort.description: Default reasoning_effort value used when the request does not specify one. Only present when supportsReasoningEffort is true.

supportsResponseSchema.description: Does the LLM model support response schema? Only models that support function calling can support response_schema.

supportsMultipleImages.description: Does the LLM support multiple images in a single request? Only applicable when supportsVision is true.

maxImages.description: Maximum number of images supported per request. Only present when supportsMultipleImages is true.

supportsVideoInput.description: Does the LLM support video input?

supportsWebSearch.description: Does the LLM model support web search?

supportsLogProbs.description: Does the LLM model support logprobs parameter?

supportsTeeAttestation.description: Does the model run inside a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) with hardware attestation? When true, use GET /tee/attestation and GET /tee/signature to cryptographically verify that inference occurred inside a genuine TEE.

supportsE2EE.description: Does the model support End-to-End Encryption (E2EE)? When true, clients can encrypt prompts using the TEE public key from attestation, and responses are returned encrypted. Requires supportsTeeAttestation to also be true.

supportsXSearch.description: Does the model support xAI's native X Search (web + X/Twitter search)? When true, you can use venice_parameters.enable_x_search to activate real-time search powered by xAI.

aspectRatios.description: Supported aspect ratio options for this model. Only present for models that support aspect ratio selection.

defaultAspectRatio.description: The default aspect ratio for this model. Only present for models that support aspect ratio selection.

defaultResolution.description: The default resolution for this model. Only present for models that support resolution selection.

resolutions.description: Supported resolution options for this model. Only present for models that support resolution selection.

default.description: The default steps value for the model

max.description: The maximum supported steps value for the model

widthHeightDivisor.description: The requested width and height of the image generation must be divisible by this value.

anyOf.description: Constraints that apply to image models.
anyOf.title: Image Model Constraints

default.description: The default temperature value for the model
The default top_p value for the model
The default frequency_penalty value for the model
The default presence_penalty value for the model
The default repetition_penalty value for the model

anyOf.description: Constraints that apply to text models.
anyOf.title: Text Model Constraints

aspect_ratios.description: The aspect ratios supported by the model. Empty array means the model does not support a defined aspect ratio.

resolutions.description: The resolutions supported by the model. Empty array means the model does not support a defined resolution.

durations.description: The durations supported by the model. Empty array means the model does not support a defined duration.

audio.description: Does the model support audio generation?

audio_configurable.description: Can audio be enabled or disabled for the video generation?

prompt_character_limit.description: The maximum supported prompt length for this video model. If not specified, the default is 2500 characters.

anyOf.description: Constraints that apply to video models.
anyOf.title: Video Model Constraints

aspectRatios.description: The aspect ratios supported by this model. Omit the parameter to use the model's default setting.

combineImages.description: Whether this model supports combining multiple input images.

singleImageAspectRatio.description: If false, output dimensions match the input on single-image edits and `aspect_ratio` is ignored. Multi-image edits are unaffected. Defaults to true.

anyOf.description: Constraints that apply to inpaint/edit models.
anyOf.title: Inpaint Model Constraints

constraints.description: Constraints that apply to this model.

description.description: A human-readable description of the model and its capabilities.
description.example: Balanced blend of speed and capability. Handles most everyday tasks with reliability.

modelSource.description: The source of the model, such as a URL to the model repository.

usd.description: USD cost per million input tokens

diem.description: Diem cost per million input tokens

usd.description: USD cost per million cached input tokens (discounted rate for cache reads)

diem.description: Diem cost per million cached input tokens (discounted rate for cache reads)

cache_input.description: Optional pricing for cached input tokens (cache reads). Only present for models that support context caching.

usd.description: USD cost per million cache creation tokens (cache writes). For some providers this may be higher than input price.

diem.description: Diem cost per million cache creation tokens (cache writes). For some providers this may be higher than input price.

cache_write.description: Optional pricing for cache creation tokens (cache writes). Only present for models where provider charges for cache writes (e.g., Anthropic charges 1.25x input price).

usd.description: USD cost per million output tokens

diem.description: Diem cost per million output tokens

context_token_threshold.description: Input token count above which extended pricing applies

usd.description: USD cost per million input tokens (extended tier)

diem.description: Diem cost per million input tokens (extended tier)

usd.description: USD cost per million output tokens (extended tier)

diem.description: Diem cost per million output tokens (extended tier)

usd.description: USD cost per million cached input tokens (extended tier)

diem.description: Diem cost per million cached input tokens (extended tier)

usd.description: USD cost per million cache write tokens (extended tier)

diem.description: Diem cost per million cache write tokens (extended tier)

extended.description: Extended pricing for long-context requests exceeding the threshold. When input tokens exceed context_token_threshold, extended rates apply to the entire request.

anyOf.description: Token-based pricing for chat models. Models supporting context caching will include cache_input (cache read) pricing. Some providers like Anthropic also charge for cache_write (cache creation) at a premium rate.
anyOf.title: LLM Model Pricing

usd.description: USD cost per image generation (base price)

diem.description: Diem cost per image generation (base price)

generation.description: Base pricing for image generation. Only present for models without resolution-specific pricing.

resolutions.description: Resolution-specific pricing. Keys are resolution names (e.g., "1K", "2K", "4K"). Only present for models that support resolution selection. When present, "generation" pricing will not be included.

upscale.properties:
<2x> object
number USD cost for 2x upscale 0.02
number Diem cost for 2x upscale 0.2
usd diem </2x> <4x> object
number USD cost for 4x upscale 0.08
number Diem cost for 4x upscale 0.8
usd diem </4x>

anyOf.description: Pricing for image generation and upscaling
anyOf.title: Image Model Pricing

usd.description: USD cost per million input characters

diem.description: Diem cost per million input characters

usd.description: USD cost per image edit/inpaint operation

diem.description: Diem cost per image edit/inpaint operation

usd.description: USD cost for this duration tier

diem.description: Diem cost for this duration tier

min_seconds.description: Minimum duration (inclusive) in seconds that falls into this pricing tier

max_seconds.description: Maximum duration (inclusive) in seconds that falls into this pricing tier

usd.description: USD cost per second of generated music

diem.description: Diem cost per second of generated music

traits.description: Traits that apply to this model. You can specify a trait to auto-select a model vs. specifying the model ID in your request to avoid breakage as Venice updates and iterates on its models.

embeddingDimensions.description: The native/default number of dimensions in the output embedding vector. Only present for embedding models.

maxInputTokens.description: Maximum number of input tokens the model accepts per input string. Only present for embedding models.

supportsCustomDimensions.description: Whether the model supports reducing output dimensions via the `dimensions` request parameter. Only present for embedding models that support it.

supports_lyrics.description: Whether this audio-generation model supports lyrics input.

lyrics_required.description: Whether lyrics input is required for this audio-generation model.

supports_force_instrumental.description: Whether this audio-generation model supports the force_instrumental request parameter.

voices.description: The voices available for this model. Applicable for TTS models and voice-enabled music models. Note: each model has its own set of supported voices.

mode.description: How the upstream provider implements voice cloning. `zero_shot` re-reads the reference audio on every synthesis call and never derives a persistent voice template (e.g. Chatterbox HD). `persistent` derives a custom voice template upstream that survives across synthesis calls (e.g. MiniMax Speech-02 HD).

accepted_formats.description: Audio container formats this model accepts as a reference sample on POST /v1/audio/voices. Samples in other containers are rejected with HTTP 400 before any upload.

min_sample_seconds.description: Recommended minimum length of the reference audio sample, in seconds, for an intelligible clone.

retention_days.description: Days a `vv_<id>` voice handle remains valid against this model. For `persistent` models the upstream provider auto-deletes the cloned voice after this many days without use; each successful TTS request resets the window. For `zero_shot` models this is the storage TTL on the uploaded reference audio; the handle stops working when it expires and the user must re-upload.

voice_cloning.description: Voice-cloning capability. Only present for TTS models whose cloning endpoint is publicly available — pass the model to POST /v1/audio/voices to mint a `vv_<id>` voice handle, then pass that handle back as the `voice` parameter on POST /v1/audio/speech alongside the same `model`. Models with cloning gated behind a private alpha (e.g. MiniMax during the biometric-data legal review) omit this field for non-staff callers; the model itself still appears in the listing.
voice_cloning.title: TTS Voice Cloning

default_voice.description: Default voice for voice-enabled music models.

supports_language_code.description: Whether this music model supports an ISO 639-1 language_code parameter.

supports_speed.description: Whether this music model supports speed adjustment.

default_speed.description: Default speed multiplier for this music model.

min_speed.description: Minimum speed multiplier for this music model.

max_speed.description: Maximum speed multiplier for this music model.

duration_options.description: Available duration options in seconds for this music model.

min_duration.description: Minimum duration in seconds for this music model.

max_duration.description: Maximum duration in seconds for this music model.

default_duration.description: Default duration in seconds for this music model.

supported_formats.description: Supported audio formats for this music model.

default_format.description: Default audio format for this music model.

prompt_character_limit.description: Maximum prompt character limit for this music model.

min_prompt_length.description: Minimum prompt length for this music model.

lyrics_character_limit.description: Maximum lyrics character limit for this music model.

model_spec.description: Compact and efficient model for quick responses and lighter workloads.
model_spec.name: Llama 3.2 3B

content.description: The scraped content in markdown format

format.description: The format of the scraped content

query.description: The search query that was executed

title.description: The title of the search result

url.description: The URL of the search result

content.description: A snippet or description of the search result

date.description: The date of the search result, if available

paths:
Run text inference based on the supplied parameters. Supports multimodal inputs including text, images (image_url), audio (input_audio), video (video_url), and files (file) for compatible models. File inputs (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, TXT, etc.) are automatically extracted to text before being sent to the model. Long running requests should use the streaming API by setting stream=true in your request.
**Authentication:** This endpoint accepts either a Bearer API key or an `X-Sign-In-With-X` header for x402 wallet-based authentication. When using x402, a `402 Payment Required` response indicates insufficient balance and includes top-up instructions. createChatCompletion

The sky appears blue because of the way Earth's atmosphere scatters sunlight. When sunlight reaches Earth's atmosphere, it is made up of various colors of the spectrum, but blue light waves are shorter and scatter more easily when they hit the gases and particles in the atmosphere. This scattering occurs in all directions, but from our perspective on the ground, it appears as a blue hue that dominates the sky's color. This phenomenon is known as Rayleigh scattering. During sunrise and sunset, the sunlight has to travel further through the atmosphere, which allows more time for the blue light to scatter away from our direct line of sight, leaving the longer wavelengths, such as red, yellow, and orange, to dominate the sky's color. null assistant

Citations from web search results.
What's the scientific reason behind Earth's sky appearing blue to the human eye? And what's the real colour of the sky?
Save 30% on the shop price when you subscribe to BBC Sky at Night Magazine today!
In this article we'll look at the science behind why the sky is blue, or at least why it appears blue to our eyes.
A beautiful blue sky is the sign of a pleasant day ahead. But what makes the sky appear blue?
So, the sky appears blue because the molecules of nitrogen and oxygen in the atmosphere scatter light in short wavelengths towards the blue end of the visible spectrum. 2024-08-13T13:45:16 Why is the sky blue? | BBC Sky at Night Magazine https://www.skyatnightmagazine.com/space-science/why-is-the-sky-blue
It was around 1870 when the British physicist John William Strutt, better known as Lord Rayleigh, first found an explanation for why the sky is blue: Blue light from the Sun is scattered the most when it passes through the atmosphere.
Published: January 20, 2025 8:34am EST · Daniel Freedman, University of Wisconsin-Stout · Daniel Freedman · Dean of the College of Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics & Management, University of Wisconsin-Stout ·
The answer has to do with molecules.
It was around 1870 when the British physicist John William Strutt, better known as Lord Rayleigh, first found an explanation for why the sky is blue: Blue light from the Sun is scattered the most when it passes through the atmosphere.
When the Sun is near the horizon, its light passes through a lot more of the atmosphere to reach the Earth’s surface than when it is directly overhead. The blue and green light is scattered so well that you can hardly see it. The sky is colored, instead, with red and orange light. 2025-04-16T16:55:11 Why is the sky blue? https://theconversation.com/why-is-the-sky-blue-246393

<//chat/completions>
**Alpha** - This endpoint is currently available to Alpha testers only.
Creates a model response using the Responses API format. This is an OpenAI-compatible endpoint for Venice text models.
**E2EE Model Limitation** - E2EE-capable models are not supported on `/api/v1/responses`. Use `/api/v1/chat/completions` with the required E2EE headers instead.
**Stateless Only** - This API is stateless. Each request is independent and no conversation state is persisted between requests. You must include the full conversation history in each request.
The Responses API provides a structured output format with typed blocks for: - **Reasoning**: Thought process and reasoning chains from reasoning models - **Messages**: The main text response from the model - **Function Calls**: Tool/function call outputs - **Web Search**: Results from web search if enabled
This endpoint supports streaming via Server-Sent Events when `stream: true` is set.
**Authentication:** This endpoint accepts either a Bearer API key or an `X-Sign-In-With-X` header for x402 wallet-based authentication. When using x402, a `402 Payment Required` response indicates insufficient balance and includes top-up instructions. createResponse

<//image/edit>
Edit or modify an image using up to three layered inputs (base image plus masks/overlays).
**Supported input formats by Content-Type:**
- **multipart/form-data**: Only file uploads are supported. Send images as form file fields.
- **application/json**: Base64 strings and URLs are supported: - Raw base64 string: `"iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAA..."` - Data URL: `"data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAA..."` - HTTP/HTTPS URL: `"https://example.com/image.png"`

</401> <402> Insufficient balance. Response varies by authentication method:
**API Key users:** Standard error response with `INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE` code. Top up your Venice balance at venice.ai.
**x402 wallet users:** Structured response with `PAYMENT_REQUIRED` code containing: - `topUpInstructions`: Step-by-step guide to top up via x402 protocol - `siwxChallenge`: Sign-In-With-X challenge template for authentication - `supportedTokens` / `supportedChains`: Accepted payment methods
The `PAYMENT-REQUIRED` header also contains a base64-encoded JSON object with the same payment requirements for protocol-level discovery (x402 v2 spec).
Base64-encoded JSON with x402 payment requirements and SIWX challenge. Present only for x402 wallet authentication. Decode to get payment instructions programmatically. false

<//characters/{slug}/reviews>

<//embeddings>
Converts text to speech using various voice models and formats.
**Voice cloning:** Some TTS models (e.g. `tts-chatterbox-hd`) accept a cloned-voice handle in the `voice` field. Mint a handle by uploading a reference audio sample to POST /v1/audio/voices, then pass the returned `vv_<id>` value as `voice` here paired with the same `model`. Supported voice-cloning models advertise the `voice_cloning` capability on GET /models?type=tts.
**Authentication:** This endpoint accepts either a Bearer API key or an `X-Sign-In-With-X` header for x402 wallet-based authentication. When using x402, a `402 Payment Required` response indicates insufficient balance and includes top-up instructions. createSpeech

<//audio/transcriptions>
Clone a voice from an audio sample. Returns a voice handle (`vv_...`) that can be passed to POST /v1/audio/speech as the `voice` parameter to synthesize speech in the cloned voice; the handle must be paired with the same `model` used to create it.
Supported models:
- `tts-chatterbox-hd` (Resemble AI) — accepts MP3, WAV, FLAC, and M4A samples. Zero-shot: no persistent voice template is derived; the reference audio is uploaded to a TTL’d storage object and re-read on each synthesis call. Handles expire automatically after 7 days.
- `tts-minimax-speech-02-hd` (MiniMax) — accepts MP3 and WAV only. Persistent: the upstream provider derives a custom voice template that survives across synthesis calls. Auto-deleted after 7 days without use; each successful TTS request resets the window. Currently limited access — contact support@venice.ai if you need it enabled.
Samples in containers outside the per-model allowlist are rejected with HTTP 400 before any upload.
**Authentication:** This endpoint accepts either a Bearer API key or an `X-Sign-In-With-X` header for x402 wallet-based authentication. When using x402, a `402 Payment Required` response indicates insufficient balance and includes top-up instructions. createClonedVoice
<$ref>#/components/schemas/CreateClonedVoiceRequestSchema</$ref>
dynamic USD 0.001 10.00
/api/v1/audio/voices Audio Voices
<200> Voice cloned successfully
Remaining x402 credit balance in USD after this request (only present for x402 auth). false
string 4.230000
object
string The cloned voice handle. Pass this to POST /v1/audio/speech as the `voice` parameter alongside the same `model` used to create it. Handles expire automatically after the per-model retention window (currently 7 days for both supported models); for `tts-minimax-speech-02-hd` each successful TTS request resets that window. vv_voice_abc123xyz
string The Venice TTS model this voice can be used with. tts-chatterbox-hd
id model false

Extracts text from a document file. Supports PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and plain text formats. Upload a file via multipart/form-data.
**Privacy:** Text parsing runs entirely in-memory on Venice's infrastructure with zero data retention. Documents are processed and immediately discarded — no content is stored or logged.

<//crypto/rpc/networks>
Proxy a JSON-RPC request to a supported blockchain node and bill per credit.
## Request shapes - **Single request**: a JSON-RPC 2.0 object (`{ "jsonrpc":"2.0", "method":"…", "params":[…], "id":1 }`). - **Batch**: an array of up to 100 JSON-RPC 2.0 objects. If any item references an unsupported method, the entire batch is rejected with 400 and the offending methods are listed.
## Supported methods Methods are classified into three pricing tiers: - **Standard (1×)**: `eth_call`, `eth_getBalance`, `eth_blockNumber`, `eth_sendRawTransaction`, `eth_getLogs`, `net_version`, `web3_clientVersion`, ERC-4337 bundler methods (`eth_sendUserOperation`, etc.), and chain-family extensions (`zks_*`, `linea_*`, `bor_*`, `starknet_*`). - **Advanced (2×)**: `trace_*`, `debug_*`, `txpool_inspect`, `txpool_status`, `arbtrace_*`. - **Large (4×)**: `trace_replayBlockTransactions`, `trace_replayTransaction`, `txpool_content`, `arbtrace_replay*`.
Stateful filter methods (`eth_newFilter`, `eth_getFilterChanges`, `eth_uninstallFilter`, etc.) are **not supported** — they break on a load-balanced HTTP proxy because filter state is pinned to a single upstream backend. Use `eth_getLogs` instead.
WebSocket-only methods (`eth_subscribe`, `eth_unsubscribe`) return 400 because this proxy is HTTP-only.
## Pricing Credits consumed per call = `baseCredits[chain] × methodTier`. `baseCredits` is 20 for most EVM chains (Ethereum, Base, Optimism, Arbitrum, Polygon, Linea, Avalanche, BSC, Blast) and Starknet; 30 for zkSync Era. The USD price per credit is `~7e-7` — a single standard EVM call costs ≈ $0.000014 and a large trace-replay costs ≈ $0.000056.
Per-request errors at the JSON-RPC layer (HTTP 200 with an `error` field in a response item) are billed at 5 credits instead of the full method tier — a small concession for methods not supported on a given chain or bad-parameter responses.
## Rate limits Two caps apply per caller: - **Requests per minute**: 100 on the paid tier, 1000 on the staff tier. - **Credits per rolling 24 hours**: 10,000,000 on the paid tier, 100,000,000 on the staff tier. When either cap is exceeded, the request returns 429 with a `customMessage` identifying which cap tripped. The per-minute cap also sets the `X-RateLimit-*` response headers.
## Idempotency Set the `Idempotency-Key` request header to any string matching `[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,255}` to enable safe retries. The response is cached for 24 hours keyed on `(user, idempotency-key)`; replaying the same key with the same body returns the cached response with `Idempotent-Replayed: true`. Reusing the same key with a different body returns 400 to prevent silent corruption.
**Authentication:** This endpoint accepts either a Bearer API key or an `X-Sign-In-With-X` header for x402 wallet-based authentication. When using x402, a `402 Payment Required` response indicates insufficient balance and includes top-up instructions. cryptoRpcProxy

Search the web and return results directly. Returns structured search results including titles, URLs, content snippets, and dates.
**Search providers:** - `brave` (default) — Brave Search with Zero Data Retention (ZDR). Search queries are never stored or logged by the search provider. - `google` — Google Search with anonymized queries. Searches are proxied through Venice's infrastructure so that your identity is not associated with the search request sent to Google. Venice does not store or log search queries.
**Authentication:** This endpoint accepts either a Bearer API key or an `X-Sign-In-With-X` header for x402 wallet-based authentication. When using x402, a `402 Payment Required` response indicates insufficient balance and includes top-up instructions. webSearch

</401> <402> Insufficient balance. Response varies by authentication method:
**API Key users:** Standard error response with `INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE` code. Top up your Venice balance at venice.ai.
**x402 wallet users:** Structured response with `PAYMENT_REQUIRED` code containing: - `topUpInstructions`: Step-by-step guide to top up via x402 protocol - `siwxChallenge`: Sign-In-With-X challenge template for authentication - `supportedTokens` / `supportedChains`: Accepted payment methods
The `PAYMENT-REQUIRED` header also contains a base64-encoded JSON object with the same payment requirements for protocol-level discovery (x402 v2 spec).
Base64-encoded JSON with x402 payment requirements and SIWX challenge. Present only for x402 wallet authentication. Decode to get payment instructions programmatically. false
